articles.title: Gensler Sticks to His Guns: Proof-of-Stake Tokens Could Be Securities – Ethereum in Trouble?
articles.description: Securities and Exchange Commission Chair Gary Gensler has once again indicated that tokens using staking mechanisms could be considered securities under US law. During an open meeting of the SEC on Wednesday, Gensler reportedly said that Proof-of-Stake (PoS) tokens can be considered securities because investors anticipate a return when they purchase them. ... Read More: Gensler...

articles.title: Ethereum’s next big upgrade Shapella expected to hit mainnet on April 12
articles.description: The Shapella upgrade is due to take place on April 12 at 10:27 a.m. UTC, with epoch number 620,9536, Ethereum developers said.

articles.title: Coinbase Issues Alert to Crypto Traders, Says It Will No Longer Support Six Ethereum-Based Altcoins
articles.description: Coinbase just issued an alert to crypto traders, warning it is preparing to end support for six altcoins built on Ethereum. The US-based crypto trading giant says it’s preparing to delist Rally (RLY), DFI Money (YFII), Mirror (MIR), OMG Network (OMG), Loom Network (LOOM), and Augur (REP). The coins will no longer be available for […]
